contract.values: float32[16] dKV contribution vector per selected route pair
contract.reason_for_probe: Test whether sorting/reducing route pairs once with a D16 vector payload avoids the scalar per-D global sort cost.

why: Pair-level route sorting and D16 value reordering avoids the worst scalar per-D sort cost: S4096 full-D512 extrapolation drops from 45.86 ms scalar random sort/reduce to 26.73 ms Vec16 random sort/reduce.

The generic rocPRIM Vec16 reduction body itself is slower than scalar reduce-by-key when keys are already grouped: S4096 sorted reduce extrapolates to 10.20 ms over D512 versus 3.54 ms for scalar reduce.
Even the better Vec16 random sort/reduce floor remains far above the current 8.6-8.8 ms sparse-atomic dKV wall and the desired fused sparse-attention backward budget.
decision.retained_lesson: Reuse or cheaply produce pair-level route ordering, but do not rely on a generic rocPRIM Vec16 reduce_by_key as the final compressed-dKV reducer.
decision.next_branch: CK-Tile/MFMA-friendly segmented reducer or split-wave/CTA grouped reducer that keeps selected-key contribution math parallel while reducing repeated compressed-group writes before global memory.
